---
title: "Virtual Reality vs. Video vs. Paper Business Case Studies: Learning Outcomes"
---

Data from a randomized experiment (N=148) comparing learning outcomes across three business case study formats: paper-based, video-based, and virtual reality (VR). Participants were randomly assigned to one condition and completed a business case study, then responded to measures of presence (IPQ), motivation (IMMS), task load (TLI), technology adoption intentions, and a knowledge recall quiz. VR participants demonstrated superior recall of both factual and visual details compared to paper and video conditions. Missingness in adoption items is structural: participants only rated adoption intentions for their assigned medium. Ideal for teaching one-way ANOVA, planned contrasts, structural missing data, and analysis of experimental between-subjects designs with multiple outcome measures.


## Source and Citation

- **Citation**: Hubbard TD. (2025). Transforming business education: the impact of virtual reality on learning outcomes in case studies. Frontiers in Virtual Reality, 6. https://doi.org/10.3389/frvir.2025.1461690
- **URL**: [https://researchbox.org/3216](https://researchbox.org/3216)
- **License**: CC BY 4.0 (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/)

## Dataset Characteristics

- **Observations**: 148
- **Variables**: 181
- **Missingness present**: TRUE
- **Longitudinal**: FALSE
- **Hierarchical**: FALSE

## Variable Dictionary

| Variable | Type | Description |
|----------|------|-------------|
| Duration__in_seconds_ | continuous | Survey completion time in seconds |
| ipq | continuous | Igroup Presence Questionnaire composite score (sense of presence in the case environment) |
| ipq1_1 | ordinal | [ITEM] IPQ (1=strongly disagree, 5=strongly agree): I had a sense of being there in the case environment |
| ipq1_2 | ordinal | [ITEM] IPQ (1=strongly disagree, 5=strongly agree): Somehow I felt that the virtual world surrounded me |
| ipq1_3 | ordinal | [ITEM] IPQ (1=strongly disagree, 5=strongly agree): I felt like I was just perceiving pictures (reversed) |
| ipq1_4 | ordinal | [ITEM] IPQ (1=strongly disagree, 5=strongly agree): I did not feel present in the virtual space (reversed) |
| ipq1_5 | ordinal | [ITEM] IPQ (1=strongly disagree, 5=strongly agree): I had a sense of acting in the virtual space |
| ipq1_6 | ordinal | [ITEM] IPQ (1=strongly disagree, 5=strongly agree): I felt present in the case |
| ipq2 | ordinal | [ITEM] IPQ awareness of real world (1=not aware of real environment, 2=still paid attention, 3=completely captivated) |
| ipq3_1 | ordinal | [ITEM] IPQ realism: How real did the case seem to you? (1=none at all, 5=completely) |
| ipq3_2 | ordinal | [ITEM] IPQ realism: How real did the virtual world seem to you? (1=none at all, 5=completely) |
| ipq4_1 | ordinal | [ITEM] IPQ involvement: How much did your experience in the case environment seem consistent with your real world experience? (1=not at all, 5=completely) |
| imms | continuous | Instructional Materials Motivation Survey composite score (motivation to engage with the case) |
| imms_1 | ordinal | [ITEM] IMMS: When I first looked at this case, I had the impression that it would be easy for me |
| imms_2 | ordinal | [ITEM] IMMS: This material was more difficult to understand than I would like it to be (reversed) |
| imms_3 | ordinal | [ITEM] IMMS: After the case, I felt confident that I knew the material |
| imms_4 | ordinal | [ITEM] IMMS: Much of the case had so much information that it was hard to pick out and remember important points (reversed) |
| imms_5 | ordinal | [ITEM] IMMS: As I worked on this case, I was confident that I could learn the content |
| imms_6 | ordinal | [ITEM] IMMS: The case was too difficult (reversed) |
| imms_7 | ordinal | [ITEM] IMMS: After working on this case for a while, I was confident that I understood the material |
| imms_8 | ordinal | [ITEM] IMMS: I could not really understand the material in this case (reversed) |
| imms_9 | ordinal | [ITEM] IMMS: The good organization of the content helped me be confident I would learn this material |
| imms_10 | ordinal | [ITEM] IMMS: There was something interesting at the beginning of the case that got my attention |
| imms_11 | ordinal | [ITEM] IMMS: These materials are eye-catching |
| imms_12 | ordinal | [ITEM] IMMS: The quality of the case helped to hold my attention |
| imms_13 | ordinal | [ITEM] IMMS: This case is so abstract that it was hard to keep my attention on it (reversed) |
| imms_14 | ordinal | [ITEM] IMMS: The case was dry and unappealing (reversed) |
| imms_15 | ordinal | [ITEM] IMMS: The way the information is arranged on the pages helped me stay attentive |
| imms_16 | ordinal | [ITEM] IMMS: This case has things that stimulated my curiosity |
| imms_17 | ordinal | [ITEM] IMMS: The amount of repetition in this case caused me to get bored sometimes (reversed) |
| imms_18 | ordinal | [ITEM] IMMS: I learned some things that were surprising or unexpected |
| imms_19 | ordinal | [ITEM] IMMS: The variety of materials helped me stay attentive |
| imms_20 | ordinal | [ITEM] IMMS: The style of the case is boring (reversed) |
| imms_21 | ordinal | [ITEM] IMMS: There was so much content that it was hard to stay focused (reversed) |
| imms_22 | ordinal | [ITEM] IMMS: Completing the case gave me a satisfying feeling of accomplishment |
| imms_23 | ordinal | [ITEM] IMMS: I enjoyed this case so much that I would like to know more about this topic |
| imms_24 | ordinal | [ITEM] IMMS: I really enjoyed studying this case |
| imms_25 | ordinal | [ITEM] IMMS: It felt good to successfully complete this case |
| imms_26 | ordinal | [ITEM] IMMS: It was a pleasure to work on such a well-designed case |
| imms_27 | ordinal | [ITEM] IMMS: It is clear to me how the content of this case is related to things I already know |
| imms_28 | ordinal | [ITEM] IMMS: Completing this case successfully was important to me |
| imms_29 | ordinal | [ITEM] IMMS: The content of this case is relevant to my interests |
| imms_30 | ordinal | [ITEM] IMMS: The content in this case will be useful to me |
| imms_31 | ordinal | [ITEM] IMMS: This case was not relevant to my needs (reversed) |
| imms_32 | ordinal | [ITEM] IMMS: I could relate the content of this case to things I have seen in my own life |
| imms_33 | ordinal | [ITEM] IMMS: The content of this case will be useful in my future career |
| tli | continuous | Task Load Index composite score (perceived cognitive load during the case) |
| tli1_1 | ordinal | [ITEM] TLI: Mental demand while participating in the case (1=low, 5=high) |
| tli1_2 | ordinal | [ITEM] TLI: Physical demand while participating in the case (1=low, 5=high) |
| tli1_3 | ordinal | [ITEM] TLI: Temporal demand (time pressure) while participating in the case (1=low, 5=high) |
| tli1_4 | ordinal | [ITEM] TLI: Effort required while participating in the case (1=low, 5=high) |
| tli1_5 | ordinal | [ITEM] TLI: Frustration while participating in the case (1=low, 5=high) |
| tli2 | ordinal | [ITEM] TLI: Self-rated performance during the case (1=low, 5=high) |
| condition | categorical | Assigned case medium: 0=paper, 1=video, 2=VR (from administrative data) |
| condition_survey | categorical | Self-reported case medium: 1=paper, 2=video, 3=VR (from survey) |
| cond_paper | binary | Indicator for paper condition |
| cond_video | binary | Indicator for video condition |
| cond_vr | binary | Indicator for VR condition |
| paperadopt1_1 | ordinal | [ITEM] Paper adoption - usefulness (1=strongly disagree, 5=strongly agree): Using paper-based cases improves my learning performance |
| paperadopt1_2 | ordinal | [ITEM] Paper adoption - usefulness: I find paper-based cases to be useful for learning |
| paperadopt1_3 | ordinal | [ITEM] Paper adoption - usefulness: Studying with paper-based cases enhances my learning effectiveness |
| paperadopt1_4 | ordinal | [ITEM] Paper adoption - usefulness: Paper-based cases help me learn business concepts more quickly |
| paperadopt1_5 | ordinal | [ITEM] Paper adoption - usefulness: I believe that using paper-based cases is beneficial for my education |
| paperadopt2_1 | ordinal | [ITEM] Paper adoption - ease of use: I think learning to use paper-based cases is easy |
| paperadopt2_2 | ordinal | [ITEM] Paper adoption - ease of use: I find paper-based cases easy to interact with |
| paperadopt2_3 | ordinal | [ITEM] Paper adoption - ease of use: I believe I can efficiently use paper-based cases |
| paperadopt2_4 | ordinal | [ITEM] Paper adoption - ease of use: Reading and understanding paper-based cases is straightforward |
| paperadopt2_5 | ordinal | [ITEM] Paper adoption - ease of use: I find it convenient to use paper-based cases for studying |
| paperadopt3_1 | ordinal | [ITEM] Paper adoption - simplicity: I think using paper-based cases is simple |
| paperadopt3_2 | ordinal | [ITEM] Paper adoption - simplicity: I find working with paper-based cases straightforward |
| paperadopt3_3 | ordinal | [ITEM] Paper adoption - simplicity: I value the simplicity and accessibility of paper-based cases |
| paperadopt4_1 | ordinal | [ITEM] Paper adoption - intention: I plan to use paper-based cases in my future studies |
| paperadopt4_2 | ordinal | [ITEM] Paper adoption - intention: I would choose paper-based cases for my business education |
| paperadopt4_3 | ordinal | [ITEM] Paper adoption - intention: I intend to advocate for paper-based cases in my academic program |
| videoadopt1_1 | ordinal | [ITEM] Video adoption - usefulness (1=strongly disagree, 5=strongly agree): Using video-based cases improves my learning performance |
| videoadopt1_2 | ordinal | [ITEM] Video adoption - usefulness: I find video-based cases to be useful for learning |
| videoadopt1_3 | ordinal | [ITEM] Video adoption - usefulness: Studying with video-based cases enhances my learning effectiveness |
| videoadopt1_4 | ordinal | [ITEM] Video adoption - usefulness: Video-based cases help me visualize business scenarios |
| videoadopt1_5 | ordinal | [ITEM] Video adoption - usefulness: I believe that using video-based cases is beneficial for my education |
| videoadopt2_1 | ordinal | [ITEM] Video adoption - ease of use: I think learning to use video-based cases is easy |
| videoadopt2_2 | ordinal | [ITEM] Video adoption - ease of use: I find video-based cases easy to interact with |
| videoadopt2_3 | ordinal | [ITEM] Video adoption - ease of use: I believe I can efficiently use video-based cases |
| videoadopt2_4 | ordinal | [ITEM] Video adoption - ease of use: Interacting with video-based cases requires little mental effort |
| videoadopt2_5 | ordinal | [ITEM] Video adoption - ease of use: I find it convenient to use video-based cases for studying |
| videopadopt3_1 | ordinal | [ITEM] Video adoption - simplicity: I think using video-based cases is simple |
| videopadopt3_2 | ordinal | [ITEM] Video adoption - simplicity: I find working with video-based cases straightforward |
| videopadopt3_3 | ordinal | [ITEM] Video adoption - simplicity: I value the simplicity and accessibility of video-based cases |
| videoadopt4_1 | ordinal | [ITEM] Video adoption - intention: I plan to use video-based cases in my future studies |
| videoadopt4_2 | ordinal | [ITEM] Video adoption - intention: I would choose video-based cases for my business education |
| videoadopt4_3 | ordinal | [ITEM] Video adoption - intention: I intend to advocate for video-based cases in my academic program |
| vradopt1_1 | ordinal | [ITEM] VR adoption - usefulness (1=strongly disagree, 5=strongly agree): Using VR technology improves my learning performance |
| vradopt1_2 | ordinal | [ITEM] VR adoption - usefulness: I find VR technology to be useful for learning |
| vradopt1_3 | ordinal | [ITEM] VR adoption - usefulness: Using VR technology improves my learning effectiveness |
| vradopt1_4 | ordinal | [ITEM] VR adoption - usefulness: VR technology helps me visualize business scenarios |
| vradopt1_5 | ordinal | [ITEM] VR adoption - usefulness: I believe that using VR technology is beneficial for my education |
| vradopt2_1 | ordinal | [ITEM] VR adoption - ease of use: I think learning to operate VR technology is easy |
| vradopt2_2 | ordinal | [ITEM] VR adoption - ease of use: I find VR technology for educational purposes easy to use |
| vradopt2_3 | ordinal | [ITEM] VR adoption - ease of use: I believe I could become proficient in using VR technology |
| vradopt2_4 | ordinal | [ITEM] VR adoption - ease of use: Interacting with educational VR technology is easy for me |
| vradopt2_5 | ordinal | [ITEM] VR adoption - ease of use: It is easy for me to become skillful at using VR technology |
| vradopt3_1 | ordinal | [ITEM] VR adoption - simplicity: I think using VR technology for education is simple |
| vradopt3_2 | ordinal | [ITEM] VR adoption - simplicity: Using VR technology for education is uncomplicated |
| vradopt3_3 | ordinal | [ITEM] VR adoption - simplicity: I find the experience of using VR technology for learning to be straightforward |
| vradopt4_1 | ordinal | [ITEM] VR adoption - intention: I plan to use VR technology in my future studies |
| vradopt4_2 | ordinal | [ITEM] VR adoption - intention: If given a choice, I would choose VR-based cases for business education |
| vradopt4_3 | ordinal | [ITEM] VR adoption - intention: I intend to recommend the use of VR technology in business education |
| adopt | continuous | Interest in adoption composite score for participant's assigned medium |
| adopt1_1 | ordinal | Adoption usefulness item 1 (recoded to participant's condition) |
| adopt1_2 | ordinal | Adoption usefulness item 2 (recoded to participant's condition) |
| adopt1_3 | ordinal | Adoption usefulness item 3 (recoded to participant's condition) |
| adopt1_4 | ordinal | Adoption usefulness item 4 (recoded to participant's condition) |
| adopt1_5 | ordinal | Adoption usefulness item 5 (recoded to participant's condition) |
| adopt2_1 | ordinal | Adoption ease-of-use item 1 (recoded to participant's condition) |
| adopt2_2 | ordinal | Adoption ease-of-use item 2 (recoded to participant's condition) |
| adopt2_3 | ordinal | Adoption ease-of-use item 3 (recoded to participant's condition) |
| adopt2_4 | ordinal | Adoption ease-of-use item 4 (recoded to participant's condition) |
| adopt2_5 | ordinal | Adoption ease-of-use item 5 (recoded to participant's condition) |
| adopt3_1 | ordinal | Adoption simplicity item 1 (recoded to participant's condition) |
| adopt3_2 | ordinal | Adoption simplicity item 2 (recoded to participant's condition) |
| adopt3_3 | ordinal | Adoption simplicity item 3 (recoded to participant's condition) |
| adopt4_1 | ordinal | Adoption intention item 1 (recoded to participant's condition) |
| adopt4_2 | ordinal | Adoption intention item 2 (recoded to participant's condition) |
| adopt4_3 | ordinal | Adoption intention item 3 (recoded to participant's condition) |
| whathappening | text | Open-ended recall: Please describe what is happening in the business case |
| product | text | Open-ended recall: What product do they sell? |
| priorrev | text | Open-ended recall: What was the revenue last quarter? |
| priorprofit | text | Open-ended recall: What was the profitability last quarter? |
| currentrev | text | Open-ended recall: What was the revenue this quarter? |
| currentprofit | text | Open-ended recall: What was the profitability this quarter? |
| factory | text | Open-ended recall: Describe the factory |
| cycletimeprior | text | Open-ended recall: What was the cycle time last quarter? |
| cycletimecurrent | text | Open-ended recall: What was the cycle time this quarter? |
| floorfactorycolor | text | Open-ended visual recall: What color was the factory floor? |
| storeceilingcolor | text | Open-ended visual recall: What color was the store ceiling? |
| pricedecrease | text | Open-ended recall: By how much does the sales team want to decrease the price? |
| currentfeaturesmonth | text | Open-ended recall: How many features does the sales team currently provide to engineering per month? |
| officedescription | text | Open-ended visual recall: Describe the corporate offices where the case discussion ended |
| floor | text | Open-ended recall: On what floor of the building were the offices? |
| confroom | text | Open-ended visual recall: Describe the conference room where the case began |
| height | text | Open-ended visual recall: How tall was the CEO? |
| total_recall | count | Total recollection of facts (composite of all recall items) |
| num_recall | count | Total numbers recalled correctly |
| try_visual_recall | count | Number of visual dimensions participants attempted to describe |
| visual_recall | count | Count of accurate visual details recalled |
| len_whathappening | continuous | Written length of the overall case description response |
| len_factory | continuous | Written length of factory description response |
| len_officedescription | continuous | Written length of office description response |
| len_confroom_info | continuous | Written length of conference room description response |
| len_all_descriptions | continuous | Total written length across all open-ended description responses |
| if_product | binary | Correctly recalled what product the company sells (0=no, 1=yes) |
| if_prior_rev | binary | Correctly recalled prior quarter revenue (0=no, 1=yes) |
| if_prior_profit | binary | Correctly recalled prior quarter profitability (0=no, 1=yes) |
| if_current_rev | binary | Correctly recalled current quarter revenue (0=no, 1=yes) |
| if_current_profit | binary | Correctly recalled current quarter profitability (0=no, 1=yes) |
| if_prior_cycle | binary | Correctly recalled prior cycle time (0=no, 1=yes) |
| if_current_cycle | binary | Correctly recalled current cycle time (0=no, 1=yes) |
| if_factory_floor_color | binary | Correctly recalled factory floor color (0=no, 1=yes) |
| if_store_color | binary | Correctly recalled store ceiling color (0=no, 1=yes) |
| if_price_decrease | binary | Correctly recalled price decrease amount (0=no, 1=yes) |
| if_current_features | binary | Correctly recalled number of features provided per month (0=no, 1=yes) |
| if_confroom_info | binary | Correctly described the conference room (0=no, 1=yes) |
| if_try_floor_number | binary | Attempted to answer the floor number question (0=no, 1=yes) |
| if_responded_height | binary | Attempted to estimate the CEO's height (0=no, 1=yes) |
| floor_number | continuous | Numeric floor number provided by participant |
| total_inches | continuous | Total estimated CEO height in inches |
| height_diff | continuous | Signed difference between estimated and actual CEO height (inches) |
| abs_height_diff | continuous | Absolute difference between estimated and actual CEO height (inches) |
| gender | categorical | Self-described gender (selected choice) |
| gender_4_TEXT | text | Self-described gender (free text, for those who chose 'prefer to self-describe') |
| if_female | binary | Female participant indicator (0=no, 1=yes) |
| race_1 | binary | Race: White or Caucasian (0=no, 1=yes) |
| race_2 | binary | Race: Black or African American (0=no, 1=yes) |
| race_3 | binary | Race: American Indian/Native (0=no, 1=yes) |
| race_4 | binary | Race: Asian (0=no, 1=yes) |
| race_5 | binary | Race: Native Hawaiian or Other Pacific Islander (0=no, 1=yes) |
| race_6 | binary | Race: Other (0=no, 1=yes) |
| race_7 | binary | Race: Prefer not to say (0=no, 1=yes) |
| if_non_white | binary | Non-white participant indicator (0=no, 1=yes) |
| hispanic | binary | Spanish, Hispanic, or Latino origin (0=no, 1=yes) |
| age | continuous | Participant age in years |
| _merge | categorical | Matching result from data merge (Stata merge indicator) |

## Suggested Learning Objectives

- Conduct a one-way ANOVA comparing three experimental conditions
- Use planned contrasts to test specific hypotheses about VR vs. paper and video
- Distinguish between structural missingness (by design) and problematic missingness
- Interpret multiple dependent variables from a single experiment

## Suggested Classroom Activities

- Compare mean knowledge recall scores across conditions and test for significant differences
- Discuss why adoption items are missing for non-assigned conditions and whether this is a problem
- Explore whether presence (IPQ) mediates the effect of condition on learning outcomes

## Common Student Misconceptions

- Treating structural NAs in condition-specific adoption items as data quality problems
- Ignoring multiple comparisons when testing all pairwise differences between three conditions

## Load This Dataset in R

```r
library(statsdata)
data <- load_dataset("vr-business-education")
```
